Construct an inference-engine specification

Source: R/fb_engine.R
fb_engine.Rd

Names a concrete inference engine and its tuning options. The result is passed as the backend argument of the fitting verbs: flexybayes(..., backend = fb_engine("greta", chains = 4L)). The bare string form (backend = "greta") remains valid and is equivalent to the default fb_engine() for that engine.

Usage

fb_engine(name, opts = list(), ...)

Arguments

name

Character(1): the engine, one of "greta", "inla", "brms".

opts

Named list of tuning options. Recognised names are n_samples, warmup, chains; an unrecognised name is an error.

...

Tuning options given individually, merged into opts (e.g. fb_engine("greta", chains = 4L)).

Value

An fb_engine object: a classed list with elements name, paradigm (one of mcmc, laplace, vb, map), toolchain_status (one of ready, requires_install, unavailable), and opts.

Details

name and the derived paradigm are closed vocabularies. "auto" is a routing directive, not an engine; pass backend = "auto" directly for automatic routing.

See also

fb_approx()

Examples

e <- fb_engine("greta", chains = 4L)
e$paradigm
#> [1] "mcmc"
e$toolchain_status
#> [1] "requires_install"
